description: Substantive quality improvements: security hardening (rate limiting, input sanitization), UX upgrades (error boundaries, optimistic mutations, form validation), performance (memoization), and code deduplication.

userStories.title: Add rate limiting to /reset-password endpoint
userStories.description: backend/auth/router.py line 270: the /reset-password endpoint lacks @limiter.limit() even though /forgot-password (line 260) has @limiter.limit(settings.rate_limit_auth). This allows brute-force token guessing. Add the same rate limiter decorator.
acceptanceCriteria: /reset-password endpoint has @limiter.limit(settings.rate_limit_auth) decorator
Rate limiting behavior matches /forgot-password
All backend tests pass: pytest tests/ -x
userStories.notes: Security fix — HIGH severity. Simple one-line change.

userStories.title: Add MongoDB operator injection validation to query filters
userStories.description: backend/applications/service.py line 54-61: user-supplied stage, company_type, and tags values are inserted directly into MongoDB filters. While Pydantic validates types, strings starting with '$' could be used for operator injection. Add a validator to ApplicationListQuery in applications/schemas.py that rejects any string field value starting with '$'.
acceptanceCriteria: ApplicationListQuery schema validates that stage and company_type do not start with '$'
Tags list items are validated to not start with '$'
A test exists proving '$ne' or similar operator strings are rejected with 422
All backend tests pass: pytest tests/ -x
userStories.notes: Security fix — MEDIUM severity. Defense in depth against NoSQL injection.

userStories.title: Wrap App.jsx routes with ErrorBoundary for per-route error isolation
userStories.description: frontend/src/App.jsx renders <Routes> without an ErrorBoundary wrapper. The only ErrorBoundary is at the app root in main.jsx line 40, which crashes the entire app on any component error. Wrap the <Routes> block in App.jsx with <ErrorBoundary> so individual page crashes show a recovery UI instead of blanking the whole app. Include a 'Go to Dashboard' recovery link.
acceptanceCriteria: App.jsx wraps <Routes> with <ErrorBoundary> component
ErrorBoundary renders a user-friendly fallback with a link to dashboard
A thrown error in one page does not crash the entire app
npm test passes all tests
npm run build succeeds
userStories.notes: UX resilience — prevents full app crashes from isolated component errors.

userStories.title: Add optimistic updates to useUpdateApplication mutation
userStories.description: frontend/src/hooks/useApplications.js lines 81-90: useUpdateApplication() only invalidates queries on success, causing a visible delay when changing application status. Implement optimistic updates: onMutate should update the cache immediately with setQueryData, store previous data, and onError should roll back to previous data. Follow the pattern already used in useKanbanDrag.js lines 27-40.
acceptanceCriteria: useUpdateApplication mutation uses onMutate to optimistically update cached data
onError rolls back to previous data from onMutate snapshot
onSettled still calls invalidateQueries to ensure eventual consistency
Status changes in DetailPanel feel instant (no loading delay)
npm test passes all tests
userStories.notes: UX snappiness — status changes should feel instant.

userStories.title: Add client-side email validation and password strength feedback to RegisterForm
userStories.description: frontend/src/components/RegisterForm.jsx: email validation relies only on browser type='email' (line 26), and password field has no strength feedback (line 32, just placeholder text). Add: (1) real-time email format validation with inline error message, (2) password strength indicator showing requirements (8+ chars, uppercase, number) with visual feedback as user types. Use the existing form validation pattern from ManualAddForm.
acceptanceCriteria: Email field shows inline error for invalid format on blur
Password field shows strength requirements with checkmarks as criteria are met
Submit button is disabled until email is valid and password meets minimum requirements
npm test passes all tests
userStories.notes: UX improvement — helps users succeed on first attempt.

userStories.title: Memoize NavBar computed values to prevent unnecessary re-renders
userStories.description: frontend/src/components/NavBar.jsx lines 131-132: offersLink object and navLinks array are recreated on every render, causing DesktopNavLinks and MobileMenu child components to re-render unnecessarily. Wrap offersLink in useMemo (dependency: none, it's static) and navLinks in useMemo (dependency: hasOffers).
acceptanceCriteria: offersLink and navLinks are wrapped in useMemo with correct dependency arrays
DesktopNavLinks and MobileMenu do not re-render when parent state changes unrelated to navigation
npm test passes all tests
userStories.notes: Performance — NavBar renders on every route change and auth state update.

userStories.title: Extract shared currency formatter to lib/currencyUtils.js
userStories.description: Identical Intl.NumberFormat('en-US', { style: 'currency', currency: 'USD' }) is defined in both frontend/src/components/OfferDetailsSection.jsx (lines 10-14) and frontend/src/pages/OfferComparison.jsx (lines 13-17). Extract to a formatUSD() function in frontend/src/lib/currencyUtils.js and import in both files.
acceptanceCriteria: frontend/src/lib/currencyUtils.js exports formatUSD(amount) function
OfferDetailsSection.jsx imports and uses formatUSD instead of local formatter
OfferComparison.jsx imports and uses formatUSD instead of local formatter
No duplicate Intl.NumberFormat definitions for USD remain
npm test passes all tests
